---
title: "Moralized Language Predicts Hate Speech (Main Paper)"
author: KS
date: '`r format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y")`'
header-includes:
   - \usepackage{dcolumn}
   - \usepackage{booktabs}
   - \usepackage{float}
   - \usepackage{longtable}
   - \usepackage{multirow}
   - \usepackage{siunitx}
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
    keep_tex: no
    number_sections: yes

---

```{r library, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(texreg)
library(xtable)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(wesanderson)
library(broom)
library(broom.mixed)
library(car)
library(furrr)

options(xtable.comment = FALSE)
options(width = 80)

plan(multisession, workers = parallel::detectCores() / 2)

source("utils.R")
options(scipen = 999)
```

```{r Set paths, include = FALSE}
inputPath <- "../Data/"
graphPath <- "../Data/Graphs/"
```

# Functions

```{r Function to plot CCDF}
plot_ccdf_dfs <- function(df = df_combined,
                          selected = vars_ev,
                          y_min = 0.001,
                          y_max = 1,
                          label_fun_x = NA,
                          legend_position = c(0.72, 0.72),
                          breaks = c(0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1),
                          ordering = NULL,
                          colors = NULL,
                          label_x = "Moral Language",
                          xlim_n = NULL,
                          grouping_var = NULL,
                          rounding = 1) {
  plot_ccdf <- df %>%
    mutate(
      !!grouping_var := factor(get(grouping_var)),
      !!grouping_var := fct_relevel(get(grouping_var), ordering)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Dataset) %>%
    group_modify(~ ccdf(.x %>% pull(selected))) %>%
    arrange(x) %>%
    mutate(y = lag(y, default = 1)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    ggplot(., aes(x = x, y = y, group = Dataset)) +
    geom_line(size = 2, aes(color = Dataset)) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = as.character(breaks * 100),
      trans = "log10",
      breaks = breaks,
      limits = c(y_min, y_max),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      labels = ifelse(is.na(label_fun_x), function(x) {
        x
      }, label_fun_x)
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = "CCDF (%)", x = label_x) +
    theme(
      legend.direction = "vertical",
      legend.position = legend_position,
      legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "cm"),
      legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "cm")
    ) +
    xlim(xlim_n)
  return(plot_ccdf)
}
```

# Variable Definitions

```{r replacement dict}
vnames_list <- c(
  "(Intercept)" = "Intercept",
  "TextComplexity" = "Text Complexity",
  "MoralWords" = "Moral Words",
  "MoralEmotionalWords" = "Moral-Emotional Words",
  "EmotionalWords" = "Emotional Words",
  "WordCount" = "Word Count",
  "QuoteTRUE" = "Quote (binary)",
  "WordCount" = "Word Count"
)
```

# Load Data

```{r load preprocessed data}
df_regression_politicians <- read_csv(paste0(inputPath, "df_politicians.csv")) %>%
  mutate(UserID = factor(UserID), SourceHate = factor(SourceHate), Dataset = "Politicians")
df_regression_newspeople <- read_csv(paste0(inputPath, "df_newspeople.csv")) %>%
  mutate(UserID = factor(UserID), SourceHate = factor(SourceHate), Dataset = "Newspeople")
df_regression_activists <- read_csv(paste0(inputPath, "df_activists.csv")) %>%
  mutate(UserID = factor(UserID), SourceHate = factor(SourceHate), Dataset = "Activists")

df_list <- list(df_regression_politicians, df_regression_newspeople, df_regression_activists)
names(df_list) <- c("Politicians", "Newspeople", "Activists")
```

# Analysis

## CCDF Plots

```{r ccdf_moral_dfs}
plot_ccdf_moral_dfs <- plot_ccdf_dfs(
  df = bind_rows(df_list),
  ordering = names(df_list),
  colors = c(wes_palette("Darjeeling1")[3:1]),
  selected = "MoralWords",
  label_x = "Moral Words",
  xlim_n = c(0, 9),
  y_min = 0.0001,
  breaks = c(0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1),
  legend_position = c(0.3, 0.3),
  grouping_var = "Dataset",
  rounding = 10
) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8)) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 9))

print(plot_ccdf_moral_dfs)

ggsave(
  plot_ccdf_moral_dfs,
  file = paste0(graphPath, "CCDF_moral_words_by_df.pdf"),
  width = 16, height = 12, units = "cm", scale = 1.0
)
```

```{r ccdf_moralemotional_dfs}
plot_ccdf_moralemotional_dfs <- plot_ccdf_dfs(
  df = bind_rows(df_list),
  ordering = names(df_list),
  colors = c(wes_palette("Darjeeling1")[3:1]),
  selected = "MoralEmotionalWords",
  label_x = "Moral-Emotional Words",
  xlim_n = c(0, 6),
  y_min = 0.0001,
  breaks = c(0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1),
  legend_position = c(0.3, 0.3),
  grouping_var = "Dataset",
  rounding = 10
)

print(plot_ccdf_moralemotional_dfs)

ggsave(
  plot_ccdf_moralemotional_dfs,
  file = paste0(graphPath, "CCDF_moralemotional_words_by_df.pdf"),
  width = 16, height = 12, units = "cm", scale = 1.0
)
```

```{r ccdf HpU}
plot_ccdf_HpU_dfs <- plot_ccdf_dfs(
  df = bind_rows(df_list) %>% group_by(Dataset, UserID) %>% summarise(HpU = mean(ProportionOfHate)) %>% ungroup(),
  ordering = names(df_list),
  colors = c(wes_palette("Darjeeling1")[3:1]),
  selected = "HpU",
  label_x = "Mean Share of Hateful Replies (per User)",
  xlim_n = c(0, 0.2),
  breaks = c(0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1),
  legend_position = c(0.75, 0.75),
  grouping_var = "Dataset",
  rounding = 10
)

print(plot_ccdf_HpU_dfs)

ggsave(
  plot_ccdf_HpU_dfs,
  file = paste0(graphPath, "CCDF_HpU_df.pdf"),
  width = 16, height = 12, units = "cm", scale = 1.0
)
```

# Regressions Analysis

## Estimation 

<!--```{r}
regressions_main <- df_list %>% imap(., function(x, name) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Starting model for:", name, "\n")
  cat("Dataset size:", nrow(x), "rows\n")
  cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")
  cat("========================================\n")
  
  model <- glmer(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + 
      Quote + (1 | UserID),
    data = x,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmerControl(
      optimizer = "bobyqa", 
      optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e8)
    ),
    nAGQ = 1,
    subset = NULL,
    weights = NULL,
    na.action = na.omit,
    offset = NULL,
    contrasts = NULL,
    mustart = NULL,
    etastart = NULL
  )
  
  cat("✓ Completed:", name, "at", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")
  
  return(model)
})

texreg(regressions_main)
map(regressions_main, get_cis)
``` -->

<!--
```{r}
# ============================================
# DEMONSTRATION: Testing model on sample data
# Full model will use entire dataset
# ============================================

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
sample_size <- 5000  # Manageable size for demonstration

cat("=" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("RUNNING SAMPLE MODEL FOR DEMONSTRATION\n")
cat("Sample size:", sample_size, "rows per dataset\n")
cat("Full dataset size: ~336k rows\n")
cat("=" , rep("=", 50), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Create sampled datasets
df_list_sample <- df_list %>% map(., function(x) {
  x %>% slice_sample(n = min(sample_size, nrow(x)))
})

# Fit models on sample data
regressions_main <- df_list_sample %>% imap(., function(x, name) {
  cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
  cat("Starting model for:", name, "\n")
  cat("Sample size:", nrow(x), "rows\n")
  cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")
  
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  
  model <- glmer(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + 
      Quote + (1 | UserID),
    data = x,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmerControl(
      optimizer = "bobyqa", 
      optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e8)
    ),
    nAGQ = 1
  )
  
  elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
  cat("✓ Completed:", name, "in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")
  
  return(model)
})

cat("\n" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("DEMONSTRATION COMPLETE\n")
cat("Models successfully fitted on sample data\n")
cat("Same code structure applies to full dataset\n")
cat(rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")

# Show that models ran successfully
names(regressions_main)
```


## Coefficients Plot

```{r coefficients plot}
model_list <- list(regressions_main$Activists, regressions_main$Newspeople, regressions_main$Politicians)
model_names <- c("Activists", "Newspeople", "Politicians")
plot_order <- c(
  "Moral Words",
  "Moral-Emotional Words",
  "Emotional Words",
  "Word Count",
  "Text Complexity",
  "Media Attached"
)

coef_plot <- model_list %>%
  map_df(., function(x) {
    broom.mixed::tidy(x, conf.level = 1 - 0.01, conf.int = T) %>%
      filter(effect == "fixed", !term %in% c("(Intercept)", "MediaAttachedTRUE", "QuoteTRUE")) %>%
      mutate(term_display = plyr::mapvalues(term,
        from = c("TextComplexity", "MoralWords", "MoralEmotionalWords", "EmotionalWords", "WordCount"),
        to = c("Text Complexity", "Moral Words", "Moral-Emotional Words", "Emotional Words", "Word Count")
      ))
  },
  .id = "model"
  ) %>%
  mutate(model = model_names[as.numeric(model)]) %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = model_names)) %>%
  mutate(term_display = factor(term_display, levels = rev(plot_order))) %>%
  arrange(model, term_display) %>%
  ggplot(all_models, mapping = aes(x = term_display, y = estimate, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, group = model, colour = model)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = grey(1 / 2), lty = 2) +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), shape = 20, fill = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.35),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(colour = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(colour = "black")
  ) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("") +
  ggtitle("Moral-Emotional language and Hate speech (Absolute)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = wes_palette("Darjeeling1")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = wes_palette("Darjeeling1"))

coef_plot

ggsave(
  coef_plot,
  file = paste0(graphPath, "COEFS_ML_moralemotional_per_df.pdf"),
  width = 16, height = 12, units = "cm", scale = 1.0
)
```

## Linear Hypothesis Tests

```{r linear hypothesis tests}
tests <- c(
  "MoralWords = MoralEmotionalWords",
  "MoralWords = EmotionalWords",
  "MoralWords = TextComplexity",
  "MoralWords = WordCount",
  "MoralEmotionalWords = EmotionalWords",
  "MoralEmotionalWords = TextComplexity",
  "MoralEmotionalWords = WordCount"
)

map(regressions_main, function(model) {
  map(tests, function(test) {
    linearHypothesis(model, test)
  })
})
```
-->





# CODE DEVELOPMENT CHECK 3 (03/01/2026)
```{r}
# ============================================
# ALTERNATIVE MODEL SPECIFICATIONS
# Testing three different modeling approaches
# ============================================

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
# Start with 500 rows for quick testing, then increase to 2000 once working
sample_size <- 2000  

cat("=" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("RUNNING ALTERNATIVE MODEL SPECIFICATIONS\n")
cat("Sample size:", sample_size, "rows per dataset\n")
cat("Models: 1. Zero-Inflated Beta-Binomial\n")
cat("        2. Generalized Additive Mixed Model (GAMM)\n")
cat("        3. Mixed Effects Random Forest\n")
cat("=" , rep("=", 50), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Create sampled datasets
df_list_sample <- df_list %>% map(., function(x) {
  x %>% slice_sample(n = min(sample_size, nrow(x)))
})

#===============================================================================
# MODEL 1: ZERO-INFLATED BETA-BINOMIAL MIXED MODEL
#===============================================================================

# Load required package
library(glmmTMB)

cat("\n" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("MODEL 1: Zero-Inflated Beta-Binomial\n")
cat(rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")

# Fit ZIBB for Politicians
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting ZIBB model for: Politicians\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Politicians), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
zibb_politicians <- tryCatch({
  glmmTMB(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + 
      Quote + (1 | UserID),
    ziformula = ~ MediaAttached + MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + (1 | UserID),
    data = df_list_sample$Politicians,
    family = betabinomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmmTMBControl(optCtrl = list(iter.max = 1e5, eval.max = 1e5))
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Politicians ZIBB:", e$message, "\n")
  return(NULL)
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Fit ZIBB for Newspeople
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting ZIBB model for: Newspeople\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Newspeople), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
zibb_newspeople <- tryCatch({
  glmmTMB(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + 
      Quote + (1 | UserID),
    ziformula = ~ MediaAttached + MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + (1 | UserID),
    data = df_list_sample$Newspeople,
    family = betabinomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmmTMBControl(optCtrl = list(iter.max = 1e5, eval.max = 1e5))
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Newspeople ZIBB:", e$message, "\n")
  return(NULL)
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Fit ZIBB for Activists
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting ZIBB model for: Activists\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Activists), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
zibb_activists <- tryCatch({
  glmmTMB(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + 
      Quote + (1 | UserID),
    ziformula = ~ MediaAttached + MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + (1 | UserID),
    data = df_list_sample$Activists,
    family = betabinomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmmTMBControl(optCtrl = list(iter.max = 1e5, eval.max = 1e5))
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Activists ZIBB:", e$message, "\n")
  return(NULL)
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Collect ZIBB models
zibb_models <- list(
  Politicians = zibb_politicians,
  Newspeople = zibb_newspeople,
  Activists = zibb_activists
)

# Display ZIBB results
cat("\n\nZIBB Model Summary:\n")
cat(rep("-", 30), "\n")
if(!is.null(zibb_politicians)) {
  cat("\nPoliticians ZIBB:\n")
  print(summary(zibb_politicians))
}
if(!is.null(zibb_newspeople)) {
  cat("\nNewspeople ZIBB:\n")
  print(summary(zibb_newspeople))
}
if(!is.null(zibb_activists)) {
  cat("\nActivists ZIBB:\n")
  print(summary(zibb_activists))
}
```

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# MODEL 2: GENERALIZED ADDITIVE MIXED MODEL (GAMM)
#===============================================================================
# Fix: Reduce k for smaller datasets to avoid "fewer unique combinations" error
#===============================================================================

cat("\n" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("MODEL 2: Generalized Additive Mixed Model (GAMM)\n")
cat(rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Note: Using reduced k=3 for small samples to avoid errors\n")

library(mgcv)

# Function to determine appropriate k based on sample size and unique values
get_k_value <- function(x, var_name, max_k = 5) {
  unique_vals <- length(unique(x[[var_name]]))
  # k must be less than or equal to unique combinations
  return(min(max_k, floor(unique_vals / 2), 10))
}

# Fit GAMM for Politicians
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting GAMM for: Politicians\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Politicians), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
gamm_politicians <- tryCatch({
  # Calculate appropriate k values based on data
  k_text <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Politicians, "TextComplexity", 4)
  k_moral <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Politicians, "MoralWords", 4)
  k_moralemo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Politicians, "MoralEmotionalWords", 4)
  k_emo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Politicians, "EmotionalWords", 4)
  k_words <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Politicians, "WordCount", 4)
  
  cat("  Using k values:", k_text, k_moral, k_moralemo, k_emo, k_words, "\n")
  
  gam(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
      MediaAttached + 
      s(TextComplexity, k = k_text) +                    
      s(MoralWords, k = k_moral) +                         
      s(MoralEmotionalWords, k = k_moralemo) +                 
      s(EmotionalWords, k = k_emo) +                      
      s(WordCount, k = k_words) +                           
      Quote +
      s(UserID, bs = "re"),
    data = df_list_sample$Politicians,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    method = "REML"
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Politicians GAMM:", e$message, "\n")
  cat("Trying simpler model without smooth terms...\n")
  
  # Fallback: Use linear terms instead of smooths
  tryCatch({
    gam(
      formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
        MediaAttached + TextComplexity + MoralWords + 
        MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + Quote +
        s(UserID, bs = "re"),
      data = df_list_sample$Politicians,
      family = binomial(link = "logit"),
      method = "REML"
    )
  }, error = function(e2) {
    cat("ERROR in simplified Politicians GAMM:", e2$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Fit GAMM for Newspeople
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting GAMM for: Newspeople\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Newspeople), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
gamm_newspeople <- tryCatch({
  # Calculate appropriate k values based on data
  k_text <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "TextComplexity", 4)
  k_moral <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "MoralWords", 4)
  k_moralemo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "MoralEmotionalWords", 4)
  k_emo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "EmotionalWords", 4)
  k_words <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "WordCount", 4)
  
  cat("  Using k values:", k_text, k_moral, k_moralemo, k_emo, k_words, "\n")
  
  gam(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
      MediaAttached + 
      s(TextComplexity, k = k_text) +                    
      s(MoralWords, k = k_moral) +                         
      s(MoralEmotionalWords, k = k_moralemo) +                 
      s(EmotionalWords, k = k_emo) +                      
      s(WordCount, k = k_words) +                           
      Quote +
      s(UserID, bs = "re"),
    data = df_list_sample$Newspeople,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    method = "REML"
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Newspeople GAMM:", e$message, "\n")
  cat("Trying simpler model without smooth terms...\n")
  
  # Fallback: Use linear terms instead of smooths
  tryCatch({
    gam(
      formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
        MediaAttached + TextComplexity + MoralWords + 
        MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + Quote +
        s(UserID, bs = "re"),
      data = df_list_sample$Newspeople,
      family = binomial(link = "logit"),
      method = "REML"
    )
  }, error = function(e2) {
    cat("ERROR in simplified Newspeople GAMM:", e2$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Fit GAMM for Activists
cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
cat("Fitting GAMM for: Activists\n")
cat("Sample size:", nrow(df_list_sample$Activists), "rows\n")
cat("Time started:", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), "\n")

start_time <- Sys.time()
gamm_activists <- tryCatch({
  # Calculate appropriate k values based on data
  k_text <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Activists, "TextComplexity", 4)
  k_moral <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Activists, "MoralWords", 4)
  k_moralemo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Activists, "MoralEmotionalWords", 4)
  k_emo <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Activists, "EmotionalWords", 4)
  k_words <- get_k_value(df_list_sample$Activists, "WordCount", 4)
  
  cat("  Using k values:", k_text, k_moral, k_moralemo, k_emo, k_words, "\n")
  
  gam(
    formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
      MediaAttached + 
      s(TextComplexity, k = k_text) +                    
      s(MoralWords, k = k_moral) +                         
      s(MoralEmotionalWords, k = k_moralemo) +                 
      s(EmotionalWords, k = k_emo) +                      
      s(WordCount, k = k_words) +                           
      Quote +
      s(UserID, bs = "re"),
    data = df_list_sample$Activists,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    method = "REML"
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR in Activists GAMM:", e$message, "\n")
  cat("Trying simpler model without smooth terms...\n")
  
  # Fallback: Use linear terms instead of smooths
  tryCatch({
    gam(
      formula = cbind(HReplies, Replies - HReplies) ~ 
        MediaAttached + TextComplexity + MoralWords + 
        MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + Quote +
        s(UserID, bs = "re"),
      data = df_list_sample$Activists,
      family = binomial(link = "logit"),
      method = "REML"
    )
  }, error = function(e2) {
    cat("ERROR in simplified Activists GAMM:", e2$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
})
elapsed <- difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins")
cat("✓ Completed in", round(elapsed, 2), "minutes\n")

# Collect GAMM models
gamm_models <- list(
  Politicians = gamm_politicians,
  Newspeople = gamm_newspeople,
  Activists = gamm_activists
)

# Display GAMM results
cat("\n\nGAMM Summary:\n")
cat(rep("-", 30), "\n")
if(!is.null(gamm_politicians)) {
  cat("\nPoliticians GAMM - Smooth terms:\n")
  print(summary(gamm_politicians)$s.table)
}
if(!is.null(gamm_newspeople)) {
  cat("\nNewspeople GAMM - Smooth terms:\n")
  print(summary(gamm_newspeople)$s.table)
}
if(!is.null(gamm_activists)) {
  cat("\nActivists GAMM - Smooth terms:\n")
  print(summary(gamm_activists)$s.table)
}
```

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# TWO-STEP RANDOM FOREST + MIXED EFFECTS
#===============================================================================

library(ranger)
library(lme4)

fit_ranger_plus_re <- function(data, dataset_name) {
  
  cat("\nFitting Ranger+RE for:", dataset_name, "\n")
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  
  # Prepare data
  x_rf <- data %>%
    mutate(
      MediaAttached = as.numeric(MediaAttached),
      Quote = as.numeric(Quote)
    )
  
  # Step 1: Random forest with importance
  rf_model <- ranger(
    formula = ProportionOfHate ~ MediaAttached + TextComplexity + 
      MoralWords + MoralEmotionalWords + EmotionalWords + WordCount + Quote,
    data = x_rf,
    num.trees = 500,
    importance = "impurity",
    case.weights = x_rf$Replies / mean(x_rf$Replies),
    seed = 123
  )
  
  # Step 2: Mixed model on residuals
  x_rf$rf_pred <- predictions(rf_model)
  lmm_model <- lmer(ProportionOfHate ~ rf_pred + (1 | UserID), data = x_rf)
  
  cat("✓ Completed in", round(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"), 2), "minutes\n")
  
  return(list(forest = rf_model, mixed_model = lmm_model))
}

# Fit models
two_step_models <- list(
  Politicians = fit_ranger_plus_re(df_list_sample$Politicians, "Politicians"),
  Newspeople = fit_ranger_plus_re(df_list_sample$Newspeople, "Newspeople"),
  Activists = fit_ranger_plus_re(df_list_sample$Activists, "Activists")
)

# Display importance
for(name in names(two_step_models)) {
  cat("\n", name, ":\n", sep = "")
  imp <- two_step_models[[name]]$forest$variable.importance
  imp_df <- data.frame(variable = names(imp), importance = as.numeric(imp)) %>%
    arrange(desc(importance)) %>%
    mutate(pct = importance / sum(importance) * 100)
  print(imp_df)
  
  # Random effects
  var_comp <- VarCorr(two_step_models[[name]]$mixed_model)
  cat("Random effect variance:", round(as.numeric(var_comp$UserID), 6), "\n")
}
```

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# MODEL COMPARISON - INCLUDING RANDOM FOREST
#===============================================================================

cat("\n" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
cat("MODEL COMPARISON\n")
cat(rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")

comparison_df <- tibble(
  Dataset = character(),
  Model = character(),
  AIC = numeric(),
  LogLik = numeric(),
  RMSE = numeric(),
  R2 = numeric()
)

# Add ZIBB models
for(name in names(zibb_models)) {
  if(!is.null(zibb_models[[name]])) {
    # For ZIBB, we can't easily get RMSE, so leave as NA
    comparison_df <- bind_rows(
      comparison_df,
      tibble(
        Dataset = name,
        Model = "ZIBB",
        AIC = AIC(zibb_models[[name]]),
        LogLik = as.numeric(logLik(zibb_models[[name]])),
        RMSE = NA,
        R2 = NA
      )
    )
  }
}

# Add GAMM models
for(name in names(gamm_models)) {
  if(!is.null(gamm_models[[name]])) {
    # For GAMM, we can calculate RMSE on training data
    pred <- predict(gamm_models[[name]], type = "response")
    actual <- with(df_list_sample[[name]], HReplies / Replies)
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
    r2 <- cor(actual, pred, use = "complete.obs")^2
    
    comparison_df <- bind_rows(
      comparison_df,
      tibble(
        Dataset = name,
        Model = "GAMM",
        AIC = AIC(gamm_models[[name]]),
        LogLik = as.numeric(logLik(gamm_models[[name]])),
        RMSE = rmse,
        R2 = r2
      )
    )
  }
}

# Add Random Forest models (from your two-step approach)
for(name in names(two_step_models)) {
  if(!is.null(two_step_models[[name]])) {
    rf_model <- two_step_models[[name]]$forest
    
    comparison_df <- bind_rows(
      comparison_df,
      tibble(
        Dataset = name,
        Model = "Random Forest",
        AIC = NA,  # RF doesn't have AIC
        LogLik = NA,  # RF doesn't have logLik
        RMSE = sqrt(rf_model$prediction.error),  # RMSE from RF
        R2 = rf_model$r.squared  # R² from RF
      )
    )
  }
}

# Print comparison
if(nrow(comparison_df) > 0) {
  cat("\nModel Comparison:\n")
  print(comparison_df %>% 
          arrange(Dataset, Model) %>%
          group_by(Dataset) %>%
          mutate(
            Best_AIC = ifelse(!is.na(AIC) & AIC == min(AIC, na.rm = TRUE), "***", ""),
            Best_RMSE = ifelse(!is.na(RMSE) & RMSE == min(RMSE, na.rm = TRUE), "***", ""),
            Best_R2 = ifelse(!is.na(R2) & R2 == max(R2, na.rm = TRUE), "***", "")
          ) %>%
          select(Dataset, Model, AIC, RMSE, R2, Best_RMSE, Best_R2))
}

cat("\n" , rep("=", 50), "\n", sep = "")
```
